aliases: Almighty Latin King and Queen Nation
description: The Latin Kings is a predominantly Hispanic street and prison gang founded in Chicago in 1954 by Puerto Ricans. It operates under two main factions: the King Motherland Chicago (KMC) and the Bloodline, with a total membership estimated between 22,000 and 42,500. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, assault, burglary, homicide, identity theft, and money laundering, with primary income from street-level narcotics distribution. It has a presence in 31 U.S. states and several other countries.

country_links.evidence_quote: Nine members of the Latin Kings in Toronto were arrested in October 2009 under the Immigration Act and Immigration and Refugee Protection Act after a two-year police sting known as Project Royal Flush.
country_links.context: The Latin Kings have a branch in Canada, founded in 2003, and members have been arrested and deported from Canada.

country_links.evidence_quote: Presence also in Canada, Ecuador, Italy and Spain
country_links.context: The Latin Kings have a presence in Ecuador.

country_links.evidence_quote: Presence also in Canada, Ecuador, Italy and Spain
country_links.context: The Latin Kings have a presence in Italy.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Latin Kings in Spain were founded in Madrid in February 2002 by Ecuadorian national Eric Javier Vara Velastegui. Branches then followed in Barcelona , Valencia and Murcia .
country_links.context: The Latin Kings have a presence in Spain, with branches in Madrid, Barcelona, Valencia, and Murcia, and were registered as a cultural association in Catalonia.
